column1: DENGUE CASE REPORTING During  the  25th  week,  5267  suspected  dengue  cases were  reported  across  24  districts,  compared  to  4819 cases in the previous week, reflecting an 9.3% increase. The  Western  Province  accounted  for  57.1%  of  total cases,  with the  Colombo  Municipal  Council (CMC) contributing 3.7%, the rest of Colombo District 20.9%, Gampaha  District  23.9%,  and  Kalutara  District  8.6%. Outside the Western Province, notable case contributions were reported from Kandy (8.4%), Matara (7.2%),  Galle  (4.7%),  Hambantota  (3.5%),  Ratnapura (3%), Puttalam (2.5%), and Kegalle (2.5%) (see Table 1). WEEKLY DISTRIBUTION OF DENGUE PATIENTS in 2024, 2025 and 2026

Source: NaDSys – National Dengue Surveillance System HIGH RISK MOH AREAS  As presented in Table 2, during the 25th week of 2026, 124  Medical  Officer  of  Health (MOH) areas  were identified as high-risk, including 25 newly flagged areas (highlighted in light red in Table 2). Previously classified 13 high-risk MOH areas in week 24, were not identified as high-risk in the 25th week of 2026 (Table 2). MIDNIGHT TOTAL OF DENGUE PATIENT ADMISSIONS *  After  incorporating  delayed  hospital  reports,  the average midnight total for the 24th week increased from 1590 to 1651. In the 25th week of 2026, the average midnight total of dengue patients across 74 sentinel hospitals was 2179. Compared  to  the  previous  week,  in  the  25th  week,  53 hospitals reported  an increase,  while  5 reported  a decrease. Table  3  highlights  sentinel  hospitals  with  high  average midnight totals and changes in dengue admissions during the 25th week.
